FLI. 64( ), TG 18 S, 1988,& FLAAL 3(1), W@ 3 SO, 1989,F FLAL 190(30), i 15 T, 1989,T FLF. 913(), TG 24 FHET, 1989,W, F13M. 12(30), A 8 S, 1990,7 HL F 742 (3), @ 30 ST, 1990, HAHLSM. 23(30), A 16 Fad, 1991, WL 93(an), 7@ 21 wIad, 1991, wmHLTT 95 (80), i@ 12 e, 1992, AL, 329(2), ard@ 13 A, 1992, MALFL 475 (o), T 5 =, 1992, WmALTRL 797(30), TG | SR, 1992 WAL 386 (1), WG 28 S, 1993, TLHLFL 422 (o), e 19 ¥, 1993, WLALFL,801 (30), w31 feEer, 1993, WoELFL 176 (1), @@ 3 adE, 1996, MUFELPL 631(H), TR 31 FH, 1997, . 504(30), T@ 20 SFT, 1998, WAL 7 (1), Air@ 2 SFad, 1999, AHFLFL 682(R), THE 5 TR, 1999, AL 742( 1), e 25 faawr, 2000, WAL 72(30), TiE 6 B, 2001, WAFLH, 543, THE 22 I, 2002, L 371(3), T 17 WE 2002, WL 489(3T), ATE 9 SEE, 2002, WAL 1088(3), AW 11 TR, 2002 sk AL 849(31), AR 30 FRER, 2002, WAL 520(0), TG 1 O, 2003, WoawLH, 92(30), wirg 29 TG, 2004, WL, 448( ), TG 12 JAK, 2004, YgwT TLALH 520(317), @@ 12 377, 2004, WFLF 272(30), T 5 7, 2005, WAL 315(a0), TG 16 T, 2005, MALF. 546(3), W@ 30 3R, 2005, WAL, 46(3), W 3 WTEd, 2006, TLALE. 464( ), TE 7 3T, 2006, WUHLFL 566(31), TG 29 3T, 2007, GLHLH, 704(30), T 12 7T, 2007, WELFL 186(37), A 18 T, 2008 AT 280(37), i@ 11 s, 2008 & g Wiy ey e A MINISTRY OF ENVIRONMENT AND FORESTS NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 7th May, 2008 G.S.R. 344(E)—. In exercise of the powers conferred by Sections 6 and25 of the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986 (29 0f 1986), the Central Government hereby makes the following rules further to amend the Environment (Protection) Rules, 1986, namely :— 1. (1) These rules may be called the Environment (Protection) Third Amendment Rules, 2008. (2) They shall come into force on the date of their publication in the Official Gazette. 2. Inthe Environment (Protection) Rules, 1986,— (i) inSchedule I, at serial number 23, relating to “Sulphuric Acid (Emission of Sulphur Dioxide and Acid Mist)” for the existing entries, the following shall be substituted, namely:— -- 2 of 3 -- [T I—8vE 3(i)] WA 3T T FRER - .3 S.No. Industry Parameter Standard 1 2 3 P “23. Sulphuric Acid Emission standards Plant Limiting concentration in mg/Nm3, unless stated Plant capacity for 100% concentration Existing Unit New Unit . of Sulphuric Acid (tonne/day) Sulphur dioxide (SO ;)yupto 300 1370 1250 Above 300 1250 950 Acid Mist/Sulphur Upto 300 90 70 Trioxide Above 300 70 50 Note: () Scrubbing units shall have on-line pH meters with auto recording facility. (i) The height of the stack emitting sulphur-dioxide or acid mist shall be of minimum of 30 metre or as per the formula H= 14 (Q) (°*) (whichever is more). ‘Where “H” is the height of stack in metre; and “Q” is the maximum quantity of SOy expected to be emitted through the stack at 110 per cent rated capacity of the plants and calculated as per the norms of gaseous emission. (i) Plants having more than one stream or unit of sulphuric acid at one location, the combined capacity of all the streams and units shall be taken. into consideration for determining thé stack height and applicability of emission standards. (iv) Plants having separate stack for gaseous emission for the scrubbing unit, the height of this stack shall be equal to main stack.” ; (i) in Schedule V1, in part-D, in para I11 relating to Load/Mass Based Standards, in serial number 4, for the existing entries, the following entries shall be substituted, namely: S.No. Industry Parameter Standard Quantum Limit in kg/tonne Plant capacity for Existing New 100% Unit Unit concentration of Sulphuric Acid (tonne/day) 4. Sulphuric Acid Plant Sulphur dioxide (SO,) Upto 300 25 20 Above 300 20 15" [FNo. Q-T3pT772972007-CPW] RK. VAISH, Jt. Secy. Note:— The principal rules were published in the Gazette of India vide number S.0.844 (E),dated the 19th November, 1986 and subsequently amended vide S.0. 433 (E) dated 18th April, 1987, 5.0. 64 (E) dated 18th January, 1988, S.0. 3 (E) dated the 3rd January, 1989, S.0. 190 (E) dated 15th March, 1989, G.S.R. 913 (E) dated the 24th October, 1989, S.0. 12 (E) dated the 8th Tanuary, 1990, G.S.R. 742 (E) dated the 30th August, 1990, $.0.23 (E) dated the 16th January, 1991, G.S.R. 93 (E) dated the 2] st February, 1991, G.S.R. 95 (E) dated the 12th February, 1992, G.S.R. 329 (E) dated the 13th March, 1992, G.S.R. 475 (E) dated the 5th May, 1992, G.S.R. 797 (E) dated the 1stOctober, 1992, GS.R. 386 (E} dated the 28th April, 1993, G.S.R. 422 (E) dated the 19th May, 1993, G.S.R. 801 (E) dated the 31st December, 1993, G.S.R. 176 (E) dated the 3rd April, 1996, G.S R. 631 (E) dated the 3 1st October, 1997, G.S.R. 504 (E) dated the 20th August, 1998, G.S.R. 7 (E) dated the 2nd January, 1999, G.S.R. 682 (E) dated the S5th October, 1999, G.S.R. 742 (E) dated the 25th September, 2000, G.S.R. 72 (E) dated the 6th February, 2001, G.S.R. 54 (E) dated the 22nd January, 2002, G.S.R. 371 (E) dated the 17th May, 2002, G.S.R. 489 (F) dated the 9th July, 2002, S.0. 1088 (E) dated the 1 1th October, 2002 and G.S.R. 849 (E) dated the 30th December, 2002, G.S.R. 520 (E).dated 15t July, 2003, G.S.R. 92 (E) dated the 29th January, 2004, G.S.R. 448 (E) dated the 12th July, 2004, Corrigenda G.SR. 520 (E)dated the 12th August, 2004, G.S.R.272(E) dated the 5th My, 2005, G.S.R.315 (E) dated the 16th May, 2005, G.S R. 546 (E) dated the 30th August, 2005,G.SR.46 (E) dated the 3rd February, 2006, G.S.R. 464 (E) dated the 7th August, 2006, G.S.R. 566 (E) dated the 29th August, 2007, G.S.R. 704 (E) dated the 12th November, 2007, G.S.R. 186 (E) dated the 18th March 2008 and G.S R. 280 (E), dated the | Ith April, 2008.
